programmes.show_description: Angela Hewitt plays Ravel's Piano Concerto in G with the Polish National Radio Symphony Orchestra, in a programme including Dutilleux's Métaboles and Ravel's Rapsodie espagnole.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
The plainsong inspired theme continues with perfomances from Voces 8 and the WDR Symphony Orchestra, Cologne and there's more specially recorded chamber music by Chopin from Perth.
From Christ Church Moss Side, Manchester, with the Choral Scholars of St Martin-in-the-Fields and Diocese of Manchester Choral Scholars.
3/5. Donald Macleod explores George Gershwin's time in Paris in the spring of 1928 and the music it inspired.
Petroc Trelawny is joined by cellists Julian Lloyd Webber and Jiaxin Lloyd Webber, and pianist Rebeca Omordia, as well as London Soundtrack Festival artistic director Michael Beek.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
The London Philharmonic Orchestra performs Anna Thorvaldsdottir's CATAMORPHOSIS and Beethoven's 6th Symphony. Plus Bruch's Violin Concerto with Nikolaj Szeps-Znaider.
Kate Kennedy meets principal French horn player Robin Graham to learn what it means for a musician's relationship with their instrument when injury forces them to stop playing.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Soweto Kinch presents more of the best in jazz from the UK and around the world and shares a swinging release from the late English jazz singer Beryl Bryden.
Nelson Goerner performs Mozart's Piano Concerto no 23 with the Orchestre de la Suisse Romande, conducted by Jonathan Nott. Later, Tchaikovsky's rarely performed Third Symphony.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
The plainsong-inspired theme continues with highlights from Monteverdi's Vespers of 1610 in a specially made recording from Monaco, plus Chopin, Schumann and Falla from Perth.
4/5. Donald Macleod tells the story of George Gershwin's first experience of working in Hollywood.
Pianist Edward Leung performs live in the studio and we talk to Mark Ravenhill on directing a new production of Salome by Regents Opera.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical favourites.
From Birmingham, the CBSO plays Rachmaninov's Rhapsody on a theme of Paganini with pianist Nelson Goerner, alongside music by Hindemith and Bartok's Concerto for Orchestra.
Kate Kennedy meets Ludwig Quandt, principal cellist of the Berlin Philharmonic, to discover how an unexpected injury forced him to rethink his life and career.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Soweto shares more exciting new jazz selections including a track from the leading British jazz, reggae and soul singer Julie Dexter, and Allexa Nava presents another Flowers pick.
